description: Entity Mapping Workflow — stateful CanonicalMapper that resolves user-defined entity labels to canonical EntityHierarchy entities via exact, country-prefix, and fuzzy matching.

userStories.title: CanonicalMapper core implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the foundational CanonicalMapper class with BIO stripping, auto-resolve pass, and get_mapping() so the rest of the API can be built on top of it.
acceptanceCriteria: In mapper.py: define IncompleteMapping(RuntimeError) with a .pending: list[str] attribute and a message that includes the count of pending labels
In mapper.py: define EntityMapper Protocol with pending property, map(), resolve_interactively(), get_mapping(), render_html() methods
In mapper.py: define _Resolution dataclass with tier (str), canonical (str | None), and score (float | None) fields
CanonicalMapper.__init__(labels, hierarchy=None, fuzzy_threshold=0.80) deduplicates input labels before processing
Before resolution, strip BIO/BIOES/BILOU/BILUO prefixes (B-, I-, O-, E-, L-, S-, U-) and suffixes (-B, -I, -O, -E, -L, -S, -U) case-insensitively; original label is used as dict key
The special outside token 'O' (after stripping) is auto-mapped to None without entering the resolution pipeline, logged as [NONE] O -> None  (outside token)
Labels that merely start with B are not stripped (e.g. BANK_ACCOUNT unchanged)
Auto-resolve pass runs in order: EXACT -> COUNTRY -> COUNTRY_FALLBACK -> FUZZY

Each resolved label is logged at INFO level with its tier, e.g. '[EXACT]   EMAIL_ADDRESS -> EMAIL_ADDRESS', '[FUZZY 87%] EMAILADRES -> EMAIL_ADDRESS', '[COUNTRY] GERMAN_PASSPORT_NUMBER -> PASSPORT', '[COUNTRY-FALLBACK] NIGERIA_UNICORN_CARD -> NATIONAL_ID  ⚠ document type not recognised'
COUNTRY-FALLBACK resolution emits at WARNING level
Labels that fail all tiers are logged at WARNING as '[UNRESOLVED] {label}  — no automatic match found'
After auto-resolve pass a summary INFO line is logged: 'Resolved N/M labels automatically (K fuzzy, J country-fallback). P require manual mapping.'
Logger name is 'presidio_evaluator.entity_mapping'
pending property returns a sorted list of labels that failed all resolution tiers
get_mapping() returns dict[str, str | None] covering every input label when pending is empty
get_mapping() raises IncompleteMapping(pending) when pending is non-empty
Update presidio_evaluator/entity_mapping/__init__.py to export CanonicalMapper, EntityMapper, IncompleteMapping
ruff check passes with zero errors on mapper.py and __init__.py
Tests pass
userStories.notes: hierarchy defaults to EntityHierarchy.default() when None. Uses EntityHierarchy.canonicalize() for all resolution. EntityNotMappedError from hierarchy is caught and routes label to pending.

userStories.title: CanonicalMapper.map() programmatic method
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to supply all label->canonical assignments programmatically in one atomic batch so the workflow runs without interactive prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: map(mappings: dict[str, str | None]) validates all entries before applying any (atomic)
Raises ValueError if any key is not in the original input labels
Raises ValueError if any value is not in ALL_CANONICAL_ENTITIES and is not None
When validation fails, no mappings are applied (no partial state change)
Applies all mappings atomically on success, updating their resolution tier to MANUAL
Can override an already-resolved label (corrects the existing resolution)
Returns self to allow chaining: mapper.map({...}).map({...})
Mapped labels are logged at INFO level as '[MANUAL]  {label} -> {canonical}'
Labels mapped to None are logged as '[NONE]    {label} -> None  (suppressed from evaluation)'
ruff check passes with zero errors on mapper.py
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. ALL_CANONICAL_ENTITIES is imported from hierarchy module.

userStories.title: CanonicalMapper.resolve_interactively() method
userStories.description: As a user, I want to be prompted for each pending label with ranked fuzzy suggestions so I can resolve unresolvable labels quickly in a terminal or notebook.
acceptanceCriteria: resolve_interactively(prompt_fn=input) is a no-op when pending is empty
For each pending label, shows a warning that no automatic match was found
Shows up to 5 ranked fuzzy-match suggestions using difflib.get_close_matches at cutoff=0.40 against ALL_CANONICAL_ENTITIES, with canonical name and similarity score
Prompt accepts: a suggestion number (1-5), a free-text canonical entity name, or 'NONE' to suppress
Re-prompts on invalid input (number out of range, unrecognised canonical name) until valid choice is made
'NONE' input maps the label to None (suppressed from evaluation)
Free-text canonical is validated against ALL_CANONICAL_ENTITIES; re-prompts if not found
prompt_fn parameter is injectable (replaces built-in input) to allow non-interactive testing
Returns self to allow chaining
ruff check passes with zero errors on mapper.py
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. The prompt_fn injection is the mechanism for all tests — no monkey-patching of builtins.

userStories.title: CanonicalMapper.from_dataset() named constructor
userStories.description: As a user, I want to construct a CanonicalMapper directly from a list of InputSample objects so I don't have to manually extract the unique entity label vocabulary.
acceptanceCriteria: from_dataset(samples: list[InputSample], **kwargs) extracts unique entity_type values from all InputSample.spans
Delegates to __init__ with the extracted labels; **kwargs (hierarchy, fuzzy_threshold) are passed through
If pending is empty after construction, returns get_mapping() directly (a dict[str, str | None])
If pending is non-empty, returns the CanonicalMapper instance
ruff check passes with zero errors on mapper.py
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. InputSample is imported from presidio_evaluator.data_objects.

userStories.title: CanonicalMapper.render_html() visual audit table
userStories.description: As a user, I want to call render_html() on the mapper to see a styled table of all labels, their resolution tier, canonical target, and score so I can audit the mapping at any point.
acceptanceCriteria: render_html() renders via IPython.display.HTML in Jupyter environments; falls back to print() plain-text in non-Jupyter environments
Never raises an exception regardless of environment
Columns: Raw Label | Tier badge | Canonical | Score
Row sort order: PENDING -> COUNTRY_FALLBACK -> FUZZY -> EXACT/COUNTRY/MANUAL -> NONE
Tier badge colours (inline styles): EXACT/COUNTRY = green, FUZZY = amber, COUNTRY_FALLBACK = amber with ⚠, MANUAL = blue, NONE = grey, PENDING = red
A summary bar above the table shows counts per tier
HTML is fully self-contained (inline styles only, no external CSS or JS)
Can be called at any point — before, during, or after resolution — to reflect current state
ruff check passes with zero errors on mapper.py
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. IPython is an optional import — catch ImportError and fall back to print().

userStories.title: Test suite for CanonicalMapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a complete test suite for CanonicalMapper so the implementation is verifiable and regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/test_canonical_mapper.py covers from_dataset() extracting unique span entity types from InputSample list
Tests cover construction and auto-resolve for each tier independently (EXACT, COUNTRY, COUNTRY_FALLBACK, FUZZY)
Tests cover BIO prefix stripping: B-PERSON resolves as PERSON; key in get_mapping() dict is B-PERSON
Tests cover suffix stripping: PERSON-I resolves as PERSON
Tests cover O token auto-mapped to None without prompting
Tests cover non-scheme labels starting with B are not stripped (BANK_ACCOUNT unchanged)
Tests cover pending content and alphabetical ordering
Tests cover map() happy path (single and batch)
Tests cover map() invalid label raises ValueError with no partial apply
Tests cover map() invalid canonical raises ValueError with no partial apply
Tests cover map() override of already-resolved label
Tests cover get_mapping() raises IncompleteMapping when pending non-empty
Tests cover get_mapping() returns correct dict when complete
Tests cover resolve_interactively() number selection, NONE keyword, free-text, re-prompt on invalid input
Tests cover resolve_interactively() is a no-op when pending is empty
Tests cover render_html() does not raise when IPython is unavailable
Tests cover CanonicalMapper satisfies EntityMapper Protocol (isinstance check)
Tests cover IncompleteMapping stores pending list and includes count in message
All tests are non-interactive: use prompt_fn injection, no monkey-patching of builtins
ruff check passes with zero errors on tests/test_canonical_mapper.py
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 through US-005. from_dataset eager-return tested with both happy path (dict returned) and pending path (instance returned).
